title: Handle Location map initialization failures
description: LocationPanel currently constructs a MapLibre map without a component-level failure path. A macOS user hit a MapLibre stack when opening the location feature, likely from WebGL or style initialization failure. Add a regression test and keep the location panel usable when map creation fails.
acceptance_criteria: Opening the location panel does not trip the global ErrorBoundary when MapLibre map initialization throws. The panel shows a localized degraded-map message while search and navigation controls remain mounted. Focused frontend regression tests cover the failure path.
close_reason: Fixed the macOS location-panel crash path by allowing MapLibre blob workers through Tauri WebKit CSP fallback, adding LocationTab degraded-map handling for MapLibre initialization failures, and covering both paths with frontend regression tests. Verified with npx tsc --noEmit and npm test.

title: Deepen canvas query and design file composition seams
description: Improve long-term architecture health by moving canvas scene freshness behind the Canvas Query Surface and driving Design file save composition from generated field ownership instead of handwritten duplicate field knowledge.
design: Keep runtime interfaces as small as practical; expose framework-specific reactivity through a thin app/runtime adapter where needed. Use generated Design field ownership as the source for composition, with explicit shared-extra handling for guides.
acceptance_criteria: Production app code no longer imports runtime mirror revision signals directly; Canvas Query Surface exposes scene read freshness through its seam; Design file composition uses generated ownership metadata to merge document-owned, scene-owned, and shared fields; source-boundary and focused regression tests cover the new invariants.
close_reason: Shipped Canvas Query Surface revision signals for scene, plant-name, and viewport freshness; moved app callers off runtime mirror revisions; changed Design file save composition to use generated field ownership with explicit shared extra ownership; added source-boundary and regression coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test -- document-format-contract frontend-boundaries-sources planning-projection canvas-runtime-surfaces maplibre-canvas-surface canvas-map-surface-lifecycle; cd desktop/web && npm test -- src/canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts src/canvas/runtime/scene-runtime/transactions.test.ts consortium-sync-workflow budget-workbench budget-tab-hover budget-tab-export consortium-chart-hover; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Deepen Scene Edit canvas mutation seam
description: Move canvas-owned persisted state changes behind a runtime-owned Scene Edit module so plants, zones, annotations, object groups, Layers, plant species colors, and guides share one mutation path for undo/redo, dirty state, mirror projections, revisions, and render invalidation.
design: Long-term health preference: make Scene Edit the only canvas mutation module even if this requires moving more code and tests.
acceptance_criteria: Scene-owned Layer and guide writes enter through the canvas runtime command/edit seam; signal-backed Layer and guide state is projection or transient UI state; focused tests cover undo/redo, dirty state, save composition, and render invalidation; agent docs reflect the new seam.
close_reason: Scene-owned layer and guide mutations now flow through the runtime Scene Edit seam; projections no longer author document state. Verified with npx tsc --noEmit and full desktop/web npm test.

title: Deepen app command, template import, and species read modules
description: Refactor three selected architecture review candidates: deepen the app command graph shared by menu/palette/shortcuts, move Design Template import coordination into a workflow module, and deepen Species Catalog read behavior used by Site Adaptation.
design: Favor long-term locality and leverage over minimum diff. Preserve existing behavior while reducing leakage through small adapter seams.
acceptance_criteria: App command metadata and dispatch are centralized behind one module; Design Template import is coordinated outside Community catalog state and core Design Session actions; Site Adaptation no longer owns raw Species Catalog SQL/storage facts for compatibility and replacement reads; focused tests cover each seam; relevant agent docs are updated or explicitly confirmed unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented App Command Graph, Design Template import workflow, and Species Catalog Read refactors; ran frontend typecheck, full frontend tests, rustfmt check, clippy, cargo check, and cargo test.

title: Deepen Species Catalog Workbench seam
description: Long-term architecture refactor from the architecture review: make the Species Catalog Workbench the public read/write module for Species Catalog UI behavior. The current Workbench protects components from some state imports, but its interface still mirrors raw search, favorites, dynamic option, detail selection, and view-mode implementation state.
design: Move Species Catalog UI state ownership into the Workbench implementation. Keep Preact ergonomics by exposing read-only reactive projections and commands from the Workbench. Stop treating app/plant-browser/state.ts as public surface; migrate components and tests away from raw writable signals except focused Workbench implementation tests.
acceptance_criteria: Species Catalog UI modules and broad tests use the Workbench interface rather than raw state exports. Workbench owns search intent/results, filter state, dynamic filter options, favorites, detail selection, view mode, and lifecycle. Source-boundary tests prevent plant-browser raw state from leaking into UI modules. Existing search, filtering, pagination, favorites, and detail-selection behavior is preserved. Required frontend typecheck and focused Vitest coverage pass.
close_reason: Implemented the Species Catalog Workbench as the public read/write seam, folded raw UI state and controller behavior into the Workbench factory/singleton, removed the old plant-browser state/controller public modules, migrated UI tests to Workbench interfaces, added boundary coverage, updated frontend agent docs, and verified with npx tsc --noEmit plus full frontend Vitest.

title: Deepen Design Session store seam
description: Long-term architecture refactor from the architecture review: make the Design Session module the read/write seam for active Design identity, dirty baselines, pending loads, saved markers, and attached/detached lifecycle state. Current Design Session behavior is deep, but tests and implementation still know too much about global signals in state/design.ts.
design: Introduce a Design Session store interface with a signal-backed adapter for production and a memory adapter for tests. Move writes for currentDesign/designPath/designName, dirty baselines, pending loads, detached canvas dirty, autosave failure, and saved markers behind the Design Session module. Preserve reactive read ergonomics through read-only projections instead of public writable globals.
acceptance_criteria: Design Session state-machine and persistence tests use a memory adapter rather than mutating global design signals directly. Production callers still get read-only reactive Design projections where needed. Existing document lifecycle behavior is preserved for attached and detached transitions, save/save-as, autosave, queued loads, and teardown snapshots. Boundary tests prevent new direct write access to Design Session internals. Required frontend typecheck and focused Vitest coverage pass.
close_reason: Implemented the Design Session store seam with signal-backed and memory adapters, migrated production callers off raw state/design imports, added a production boundary guard, updated document lifecycle agent docs, and verified with npx tsc --noEmit plus focused Vitest suites for Design Session, persistence, dirty state, document actions, and canvas document session integration.

title: Deepen Species Catalog SQL Planning
description: Architecture review found shallow Species Catalog SQL planning modules. The query builder splits useful concepts into modules, but the interface still leaks mutable parameter order, placeholder numbering, join alias ordering, count/list predicate convention, and string assembly details. Deepen Species Catalog SQL planning around a typed internal builder that owns placeholders, joins, predicate fragments, sort fragments, pagination, and shared count/list behavior.
design: Use a typed internal builder inside desktop/src/db/query_builder rather than ad hoc SQL strings passed through every module. Preserve the public SpeciesSearchRequest and SpeciesSearchPlan interface unless implementation proves a small compatibility facade is needed.
acceptance_criteria: SpeciesSearchPlan exposes the same search behavior while internal SQL assembly uses a typed plan/builder seam for placeholders, joins, predicates, order, limit, and count/list reuse. Existing backend search/filter/relevance tests pass or are strengthened. No runtime dependency is added. Rust formatting, clippy/check scope relevant to changed backend code is run or explicitly documented if blocked.
close_reason: Shipped typed query_builder SqlBuilder seam for Species Catalog SQL placeholder allocation, routed predicates/relevance/pagination/fixed filters through it, added dense-placeholder regression coverage, updated database agent guide, and passed cargo fmt --all -- --check, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::query_builder, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Deepen Planning Canvas Workbenches
description: Architecture review found shallow planning canvas modules. Timeline Action and Consortium canvas workbenches currently require callers to understand renderer geometry, pointer ordering, local view state, Target presentation, popover lifecycle, and document edit transactions. Deepen the workbench modules so canvas hosts forward events and snapshots through a smaller seam while interaction implementation owns hover, selection presentation, drag preview and commit, popup orchestration, cleanup, and local view-state ordering.
design: Use separate deep modules first. Do not create a generic planning canvas abstraction until Timeline and Consortium prove the same interface. The deep modules own local view state involved in interaction correctness, including scroll, hover, pending click, popup state, drag lifecycle, auto-scroll, and cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: InteractiveTimeline delegates full Timeline Action interaction/view-state ordering to a deep app timeline workbench module. ConsortiumChart delegates full Consortium drag/hover ordering to a deep app consortium workbench module. Existing behavior is preserved with focused tests for hover, selection, drag commit/cleanup, popover open/save/delete, and no-op cleanup. Frontend typecheck and focused Vitest coverage pass.
close_reason: Deepened Planning Canvas Workbenches by moving Timeline and Consortium interaction ordering into app-layer workbench seams, updated boundary tests and agent docs, and verified with npx tsc --noEmit plus focused and full frontend Vitest suites.

title: Deepen Species Catalog search
description: Architecture review selected this as the second long-term-health refactor. Species Catalog search policy is currently spread across frontend search-session intent, IPC argument shapes, Tauri command arguments, plant_browser orchestration, user favorite hydration, plant_db search execution, and query_builder planning. Deepen this into a structured search module with flat transports as adapters.
acceptance_criteria: A structured Species Catalog search request/result contract replaces the internal flat argument chain; frontend search-session and IPC use adapter seams; Rust command/service/search modules consume the structured contract; favorite hydration and count policy are tested through the deeper interface; generated bindings are refreshed if shared contracts change; agent docs are updated only if commands or architecture guidance change.
close_reason: Deepened Species Catalog search: common-types now owns SpeciesSearchRequest, frontend search-session and IPC use a structured request with flat Tauri adapter, Rust command/service/plant DB/query planner consume the request object, favorite hydration and count-policy tests pass, generated bindings and agent docs were updated, and CI-parity gates passed.

title: Deepen Design file contract
description: Architecture review selected this as the top long-term-health refactor. The Design file contract is currently encoded across Rust shared types, generated TypeScript known keys, frontend field ownership metadata, scene serialization placeholders, and backend migration/default behavior. Deepen this into one authoritative contract so adding a Design field is no longer a cross-module memory test.
acceptance_criteria: common-types owns budget_currency and Design file defaults; generated frontend metadata includes known keys and field ownership; save/load composition tests cover ownership, defaults, and unknown fields; generated bindings are refreshed; agent docs are updated only if workflow commands or architecture guidance change.
close_reason: Deepened the Design file contract: common-types now owns budget_currency/defaults and field ownership metadata, generated frontend contract metadata is consumed by save composition, generated bindings were refreshed, tests and CI-parity gates passed.

title: Prepare 0.5.0 release
description: Bump app/package versions to 0.5.0, trigger the release candidate workflow, and leave release notes for after the GitHub release is created.
close_reason: Released Canopi 0.5.0 from RC run 26666890609, published release notes, and verified public GitHub release assets.

title: Fix macOS canvas object creation without randomUUID
description: Users on macOS 0.4.0 report they cannot add any canvas object. Older WKWebView builds may lack crypto.randomUUID, which is used by shared canvas object-creation paths. Add a deterministic repro and replace direct randomUUID calls with a compatible frontend ID helper.
close_reason: Fixed macOS canvas object creation by replacing direct frontend crypto.randomUUID calls with a createUuid helper that falls back to getRandomValues; added a SceneInteractionController regression for missing native randomUUID; verified npx tsc --noEmit and full npm test.

title: Collapse Timeline Action workbench
description:
Refactor the frontend Timeline Action workbench so InteractiveTimeline delegates add/edit/delete popover orchestration, selection cleanup, and document mutations through an app/timeline seam instead of owning those workflows inline. Preserve existing hover, drag, and document authority behavior.

## Required Skills
craft,improve-codebase-architecture
design: Create a deep app/timeline workbench module that coordinates Timeline Action form data, controller calls, popover mode, selection targets, and action mutations behind a small interface. Keep InteractiveTimeline responsible for canvas rendering, hit testing, pointer geometry, and refs.
acceptance_criteria: InteractiveTimeline no longer imports timeline controller mutations or form mapping helpers directly; Timeline Action add/edit/delete flows preserve behavior; boundary tests document the workbench seam; focused Timeline tests plus frontend typecheck pass.
close_reason: Collapsed Timeline Action workbench orchestration into app/timeline/workbench.ts; InteractiveTimeline now delegates popover action mutations and target cleanup through that seam; frontend typecheck and full Vitest suite passed.

title: Refactor Design Session into explicit state machine
description: Refactor the frontend Design Session lifecycle so guarded document transitions, attached/detached apply paths, queued loads, save composition, dirty baseline resets, autosave, teardown snapshots, and workflow installation are coordinated through an explicit state-machine module. Preserve existing document authority behavior and regression coverage.
design: Create a deep app/document-session state-machine seam. Keep DOM runtime ownership in lifecycle.ts and user action loading in actions.ts, but move transition ordering and session state into a single module with explicit state/status values and injected adapters for persistence/workflows/dialog/IPC.
acceptance_criteria: Design Session transitions preserve existing open/new/template/queued/mount behavior; attached and detached save paths preserve canvas/non-canvas authority; autosave and teardown use the state-machine surface; focused Vitest coverage documents state transitions and cancellation/error paths; docs/agent guidance is updated if the seam changes.
close_reason: Implemented explicit Design Session state machine; lifecycle now delegates start, autosave, and teardown through it; frontend typecheck and full Vitest suite passed.

title: Generate Species Catalog Filter adapter
description: Deepen the Species Catalog Filter module by moving repeated frontend/backend fixed-filter adapter behavior into generated metadata or generated adapter code, reducing parallel manual behavior across plant-filter-fields.json, TypeScript filter model code, Rust query_builder fixed-filter code, and shared SpeciesFilter request values.
acceptance_criteria: Fixed Species Catalog Filter behavior is generated or schema-driven on both TypeScript and Rust sides; request-value and SQL predicate behavior remain covered; generated bindings are current; frontend and Rust filter tests pass.
close_reason: Generated more Species Catalog Filter adapter behavior from common-types/plant-filter-fields.json: categorical strip fields now carry strip/chip UI metadata, frontend strip/chip catalogs consume generated behavior, and the Rust fixed SpeciesFilter request-value adapter is generated beside fixed-filter predicates. Verified generated bindings, focused filter tests, full frontend Vitest, TypeScript check, Rust format/check/clippy, and Rust workspace tests.

title: Deepen Design Session lifecycle
description: Refactor the Design Session lifecycle so attach, transition, save, autosave, teardown snapshot, dirty baselines, queued loads, and workflow installation are owned by a deeper app/document-session module instead of leaking ordering across the CanvasPanel hook, transition, persistence, and state modules.
acceptance_criteria: Design Session lifecycle has a single app-facing seam for canvas mount and autosave; existing document transition, persistence, dirty-state, queued-load, and canvas-session tests pass or are strengthened; no document authority rules are weakened.
close_reason: Deepened Design Session lifecycle by moving canvas runtime attachment, mount transition, autosave scheduling, queued-load installation, settings flush, teardown snapshot, persistence disposal, and canvas-session publication behind app/document-session/lifecycle.ts. Verified with focused lifecycle/boundary Vitest, full frontend Vitest, TypeScript check, Rust format/check/clippy, and Rust workspace tests.

title: Deepen Species Catalog Filter catalog
description: Why: Species Catalog Filter behavior is partly data-driven, but fixed-filter defaults, activity rules, presentation metadata, and SQL dispatch still require duplicated caller knowledge across JSON, generated TypeScript/Rust metadata, frontend model code, and backend query builder code. What: derive fixed-filter defaults, activity, UI behavior, and predicate dispatch from a single catalog seam while preserving the flat SpeciesFilter request shape unless a later bead deliberately changes IPC contracts.
design: Keep SpeciesFilter flat for compatibility. Deepen the catalog around it first, then decide separately whether the request contract should change.
acceptance_criteria: Fixed Species Catalog Filter behavior has one catalog interface for frontend defaults/activity/presentation and backend predicate dispatch; generated bindings remain source-controlled; focused frontend and backend tests cover catalog-derived behavior.
notes: Follow-up to closed canopi-9go. That bead moved fixed Species Catalog Filter presentation and Rust predicate behavior into generated metadata; this bead should focus only on remaining catalog shallowness such as frontend defaults/activity and any backend dispatch knowledge still duplicated after that work.
close_reason: Deepened the Species Catalog Filter catalog so frontend fixed-filter defaults and backend SQL dispatch derive from generated fixed-filter behavior, with focused frontend tests, query-builder coverage, full Rust workspace tests, typecheck, format, check, and clippy passing.

title: Deepen Design Session persistence module
description: Why: Design Session save, autosave, attached/detached persistence, teardown snapshotting, and field ownership composition are spread across several modules, so callers must preserve ordering knowledge. What: introduce a deeper Design Session persistence seam that centralizes persisted Design content production and snapshot behavior while preserving existing transition and dirty-state semantics.
design: Keep transition flow intact for this slice. Deepen the seam around producing persisted Design content and snapshotting canvas state, then update callers to consume that module instead of scattered runtime helpers.
acceptance_criteria: Persisted Design content for save/autosave/teardown goes through one Design Session persistence module; existing document transition, autosave, attached/detached snapshot, and field ownership behavior remains covered by focused tests; no unrelated architecture changes are included.
close_reason: Introduced app/document-session/persistence as the Design Session persistence seam, moved save/autosave/teardown callers to it, removed the old runtime helper, updated agent docs, and verified with focused Vitest, full frontend Vitest, and TypeScript checks.

title: Fix review-found correctness bugs
description: Fix the bugs found during static review: settings persistence must not mark failed saves as clean and close must flush settings; unknown .canopi top-level fields must round-trip through frontend saves; plant search cursor pagination must handle nullable sort values; document file actions must work when the canvas panel is unmounted; redo shortcut handling must accept shifted key events.
acceptance_criteria: Focused regression tests cover each fixed bug. Frontend typecheck passes. Rust workspace check passes for backend query changes.
notes: Implemented fixes for review-found correctness bugs: durable settings persistence/close flush, top-level .canopi unknown field preservation, nullable sort cursor pagination, detached document actions, and shifted redo shortcut. Verification passed: focused Vitest suite, full npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, cargo test query_builder, cargo test height_pagination_continues_after_null_sort_value, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace, cargo test --workspace.
close_reason: Fixed all review-found correctness bugs and verified with focused/full frontend tests, TypeScript check, Rust workspace check, and Rust workspace tests.

title: Local Problem Report Flow
description:
## Slice Summary
Add the first user-visible Problem Report path. A user can create a local report package from the app and receives an easy-to-find folder containing a Report Summary and Diagnostic Bundle.

## User Value
A non-technical user has concrete files they can share through any future support channel without needing to find app logs manually.

## Out of Scope
Automatic upload, email integration, sensitive attachments, and folder reveal/open behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the first local Problem Report flow that creates easy-to-find report artifacts.

**Current behavior:**
Canopi writes backend logs and surfaces some frontend errors, but users do not have a guided way to create a Problem Report or collect troubleshooting evidence.

**Desired behavior:**
A user can start a Problem Report from the app, provide a short description, and create a timestamped local folder that is easy to locate. The folder contains a readable Report Summary and a Diagnostic Bundle with privacy-safe support evidence by default. The success state shows the folder path and supports copying the Report Summary text.

**Key interfaces:**
- Problem Report creation command or workflow.
- Diagnostic Bundle contents contract.
- Report Summary text contract.
- App menu or command registry entry for the user entry point.
- Settings, subsystem health, backend logs, and frontend-provided context as support evidence sources.

**Out of scope:**
- Automatic upload or send behavior.
- Email draft integration.
- Opening or revealing the folder in the operating system file manager.
- Opt-in sensitive attachments such as current Design contents, screenshots, or precise Location.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A user-visible command starts a Problem Report flow without requiring developer tools.
- [ ] Creating a report writes a timestamped folder with `Report Summary.txt` and `Diagnostic Bundle.zip`.
- [ ] The generated folder is easy for a non-technical user to locate, with Desktop as the preferred default when available.
- [ ] The Report Summary includes the user's description plus safe app, platform, and report metadata.
- [ ] The Diagnostic Bundle includes privacy-safe support evidence by default and excludes Design contents, precise Location, screenshots, and unsanitized filesystem paths.
- [ ] The success state shows the exact folder path and provides a Copy Summary action.
- [ ] Focused tests cover bundle creation, default privacy exclusions, and the user-facing report flow.

title: Frontend Diagnostics Capture
description:
## Slice Summary
Capture frontend runtime errors so future Diagnostic Bundles include recent UI-side failures in addition to backend logs.

## User Value
Problem Reports become useful for UI failures that currently only appear in the developer console.

## Out of Scope
Remote telemetry, analytics, user behavior tracking, and screenshot capture.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Capture recent frontend errors for inclusion in Diagnostic Bundles.

**Current behavior:**
Frontend failures are mostly written to the browser console, so a user's Diagnostic Bundle would miss UI-side errors unless a developer is present.

**Desired behavior:**
The app keeps a bounded, privacy-conscious in-memory record of recent frontend errors and handled diagnostic events. Error boundaries and command failures feed that record. Diagnostic Bundle creation can include those recent frontend diagnostics without tracking user behavior or uploading data.

**Key interfaces:**
- Frontend diagnostics collector.
- Error boundary reporting.
- Command and workflow error reporting seams.
- Diagnostic Bundle frontend evidence input.

**Out of scope:**
- Remote telemetry, analytics, session replay, or usage tracking.
- Capturing screenshots or Design contents.
- Persisting an indefinite frontend log history.
- Replacing backend logging.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Recent frontend errors are captured in a bounded diagnostic buffer.
- [ ] ErrorBoundary failures are recorded in the diagnostic buffer.
- [ ] Command or workflow failures that are already logged to the console can be routed into the diagnostic buffer.
- [ ] Diagnostic entries avoid sensitive Design data and avoid full unsanitized filesystem paths.
- [ ] Diagnostic Bundle creation can include the captured frontend diagnostics.
- [ ] Focused tests cover buffer bounds, recorded error shape, and Diagnostic Bundle inclusion.

title: Show Problem Report Folder
description:
## Slice Summary
Let users reveal the generated Problem Report folder from the report success screen.

## User Value
A non-technical user can immediately find the Report Summary and Diagnostic Bundle after creation.

## Out of Scope
Changing the report contents, adding send/upload behavior, or introducing a full file manager UI.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Let users reveal the generated Problem Report folder from the app.

**Current behavior:**
The planned local Problem Report flow can create an easy-to-find folder, but users still may need to navigate to the path manually.

**Desired behavior:**
After a Problem Report is created, the success state offers a Show Folder action that opens or reveals the generated folder using an approved native capability. The action should fail gracefully and keep the exact path visible if the operating system cannot open the folder.

**Key interfaces:**
- Native folder open or reveal command/capability.
- Problem Report success state.
- Folder path returned by Problem Report creation.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Diagnostic Bundle contents.
- Adding send/upload behavior.
- Building a custom file manager.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Problem Report success state includes a Show Folder action.
- [ ] The action opens or reveals the generated report folder on supported platforms.
- [ ] The action uses an explicit native capability or command rather than unrestricted shell execution.
- [ ] Failures are handled with a user-visible message while keeping the folder path available.
- [ ] Focused tests cover the success-state behavior and failure handling.

title: Choose Public Problem Report Channel
description:
## Slice Summary
Decide the public support destination for Problem Reports before adding any send action to the app.

## User Value
The product avoids promising a support path that does not exist yet, while preserving a clear decision point for email, form, helpdesk, or another channel.

## Out of Scope
Implementing email drafts, web forms, upload APIs, or GitHub issue creation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Choose the public channel users should use to send Problem Reports.

**Current behavior:**
The product does not yet have a public support email, form, helpdesk, or other non-technical destination for Problem Reports. Most users should not be sent to GitHub as the primary path.

**Desired behavior:**
A human owner chooses the canonical public support channel for Canopi Problem Reports. The decision should state whether the app should open an email draft, link to a web form, integrate with a helpdesk, or keep manual sharing only. Until this is decided, implementation work should not add a send action.

**Key interfaces:**
- Problem Report user journey.
- Public support destination.
- Future send/share action.

**Out of scope:**
- Implementing the selected channel.
- Adding GitHub as the primary user-facing reporting path.
- Changing the local Diagnostic Bundle privacy boundary.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A canonical public support channel is selected or manual sharing is explicitly retained.
- [ ] The decision identifies the user-facing destination users should see in the app.
- [ ] The decision states whether GitHub is hidden, secondary, or developer-only.
- [ ] Follow-up implementation beads are created if the chosen channel requires app changes.

title: Deepen frontend state seams
description: Hide low-level Design Session signals behind the public Design Session store/test seams, and deepen Target presentation so Planning Projection no longer owns hover/selection lifecycle aliases.
acceptance_criteria: Production and broad frontend tests avoid direct imports of desktop/web/src/state/design.ts outside the Design Session store seam and focused implementation tests. Canvas runtime tests use a Design Session test adapter instead of raw canvas-clean signals. Target presentation lifecycle lives behind app/panel-targets/presentation.ts with origin-aware helpers; Planning Projection stops re-exporting thin Target presentation aliases. Agent docs and domain vocabulary are updated to match the strengthened seams. Focused frontend tests and TypeScript checks pass.
close_reason: Deepened frontend state seams: tests now use a Design Session test adapter instead of low-level state/design imports, Target Presentation owns origin-aware hover/selection lifecycle, Planning Projection no longer re-exports Target Presentation aliases, and domain/agent docs reflect the boundary. Verified with desktop/web npx tsc --noEmit, focused Vitest coverage, and full desktop/web npm test.

title: Deepen Budget Item Workbench
description: Consolidate Budget Item editing, currency commands, export shaping, formatting, and Target presentation behind a Budget Item Workbench while keeping Planning Projection as read-only derivation.
design: Use the established Timeline Action and Consortium workbench shape: UI renders, workbench owns interaction behavior, controllers own non-canvas Design mutation.
acceptance_criteria: BudgetTab consumes a Budget Item Workbench interface; budget export no longer imports canvas helpers; price editing and currency behavior live under app/budget; tests cover edit commits, zero-price handling, Target hover/selection, export escaping, and projection separation.
close_reason: BudgetTab now consumes the Budget Item Workbench, which owns price drafts, currency commands, CSV export handling, formatting, and budget Target presentation while Planning Projection stays read-only. Verified with npx tsc --noEmit, focused budget/boundary Vitest, and full desktop/web npm test.

title: Deepen Location Workbench
description: Consolidate Location editing and presentation behind a Location Workbench that owns location drafts, geocoding, altitude preservation, saved-site presentation, and map-readiness cues without owning canvas scene data.
design: Depends on the Scene Edit seam so map and Layer consumers use the stabilized canvas authority model.
acceptance_criteria: LocationInput, LocationTab, CanvasPanel, LayerPanel, and map surface code consume a Location Workbench interface for saved Location state and commands; duplicated geocoding and commit behavior is removed; tests cover altitude preservation, search result commits, saved-site presentation, and map readiness.
close_reason: LocationInput, LocationTab, CanvasPanel, LayerPanel, and map surface code now consume the Location Workbench for saved Location presentation, drafts, geocoding state, search result commits, altitude-preserving map commits, and map pin/readiness helpers. Verified with npx tsc --noEmit, focused location/map Vitest, and full desktop/web npm test.

title: Deepen Planning Projection and Timeline Action Workbench
description: Planning surfaces still assemble Design Session state, canvas scene snapshots, localized Species names, Target presentation, timeline layout, stale selection cleanup, and popover inputs across multiple call sites. Deepen the Planning Projection module as the app-facing planning seam, then collapse Timeline Action Workbench so InteractiveTimeline becomes a thin canvas host. Preserve pure projection builders as implementation/test support while reducing caller knowledge.
design: Use one Planning Projection module with narrower methods rather than one oversized runtime object. Keep pure build*Projection functions for implementation tests, but move runtime input assembly and planning Target presentation helpers behind the Planning Projection seam. Collapse Timeline Action Workbench internals before changing visual behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Planning surfaces consume narrower Planning Projection runtime seams instead of assembling canvas scene and Target presentation inputs directly. Timeline Action Workbench owns selected Timeline Action identity, stale selection cleanup, timeline layout offsets, origin derivation, and popover inputs. Existing planning behavior is preserved with focused frontend tests and boundary-source tests updated or added. Agent docs are updated if the planning seam rules change.
close_reason: Implemented deeper Planning Projection surface hooks, migrated Budget/Timeline/Consortium planning callers, collapsed Timeline Action Workbench ownership of selection/layout/origin/popover inputs, updated planning seam docs, and verified with focused Vitest, full frontend Vitest, and TypeScript check.

title: Guard Site Adaptation Workbench sibling seam
description: Site Adaptation should remain a sibling Design workflow instead of becoming a Species Catalog Workbench mode. Add source guardrails and docs that keep adaptation modules from depending on plant-browser Workbench state or reusing Workbench-owned UI modules.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend source tests fail if Site Adaptation imports app/plant-browser, components/plant-db, or components/plant-detail. Agent docs state that Workbench UI modules are Workbench-owned while prop-only shared Species presentation must live outside Workbench-owned directories. Focused frontend boundary test passes.
close_reason: Added source guardrails keeping Site Adaptation independent from plant-browser, plant-db, and plant-detail Workbench modules; documented Workbench-owned UI directories and verified with npm test -- frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts plus npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Fix Species Catalog token relevance review findings
description:
Review found that query-side Common Name tokenization diverges from the prepare-db token index for punctuation and Unicode case folding, and that explicit non-relevance sorts still pay token join cost even though relevance ordering is unused.

## Required Skills
tdd,craft
acceptance_criteria: Relevance query tokens follow the same word-splitting and normalization contract as scripts/prepare-db.py common_name_tokens for punctuation and Unicode fold cases. title: Implement snappy broad-query behavior
description:
## Slice Summary

## Out of Scope

design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Implement the approved short-query and broad-query policy so Species Catalog search remains snappy.

**Current behavior:** Broad searches can spend too much time collecting candidates and counts, which makes the Species Catalog Workbench feel slow even when the first useful page could be shown quickly.

**Desired behavior:** The Workbench applies the approved short-query policy and keeps interaction responsive for broad searches. First-page results should appear quickly, count behavior should match the decision bead, and stale committed results should remain stable while a new broad search is in flight.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Species Catalog Workbench loading and pagination behavior
- Species Catalog latency harness

**Out of scope:** Do not change relevance ranking except where necessary to obey the approved broad-query policy.

title: Rank current-locale Common Name whole-token matches first
description:
## Slice Summary
Make active-locale whole-token Common Name matches rank ahead of Canonical Name and longer-token prefix matches in Species Catalog text search.

## User Value
A designer searching French `lin` sees `Lin commun`, `Lin bisannuel`, and related French Common Names before `Lindleya mespiloides`.

## Out of Scope
Do not add multi-word phrase ranking beyond what is necessary for whole-token behavior. Do not change broad-query count policy.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Rank active-locale Common Name whole-token matches ahead of Canonical Name prefixes and long-token matches.

**Current behavior:** A query such as French `lin` can rank `Lindleya mespiloides` near the top because prefix matching treats long taxonomy/name tokens as competitive with whole Common Name tokens. Runtime Common Name probes have been explored but are too slow for interactive search.

**Desired behavior:** Active-locale Common Name whole-token matches are ranked first using indexed or precomputed name data. For French `lin`, plants with `Lin ...` Common Names should appear ahead of `Lindleya mespiloides`, while explicit non-name sorts and structured filters keep their existing behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Generated Species Catalog search-name index or equivalent indexed data
- Species Catalog search locale
- Relevance ordering used by the Species Catalog Workbench

**Out of scope:** Do not implement phrase/all-token ranking, broad-query policy changes, or sort UI copy changes in this slice.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Active-locale Common Name whole-token matches rank ahead of Canonical Name prefix matches for relevance-sorted searches.
- [ ] French `lin` ranks active-locale `Lin ...` Common Names before `Lindleya mespiloides`.
- [ ] The implementation avoids per-row correlated Common Name scans at query time.
- [ ] First-page latency for representative short Common Name queries stays within the harness budget.
- [ ] Structured filters and explicit non-name sorts keep existing behavior.
- [ ] Agent docs are updated if Species Catalog schema or search-index rules change.
close_reason: Implemented active-locale Common Name whole-token relevance ranking with a generated indexed species_search_common_name_tokens table, schema v9 generator support, compatibility fallback for older DBs, focused relevance/planner tests, database docs, and verification via py_compile, prepare-db to /tmp, v9 latency harness, cargo fmt check, cargo check, clippy, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Add phrase and all-token Common Name relevance tiers
description:
## Slice Summary
Extend Species Catalog relevance so multi-word Common Name searches prefer exact phrase matches, then all-token active-locale Common Name matches, before broader prefix matches.

## User Value
A designer searching `lin commun` gets the expected exact Common Name match first, while partial multi-word searches remain intuitive.

## Out of Scope
Do not revisit the short-query/count policy. Do not alter Species Catalog Filter behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add phrase and all-token relevance tiers for multi-word active-locale Common Name searches.

**Current behavior:** After whole-token matching exists, multi-word Common Name searches can still be ordered mostly by broad prefix/token behavior. Exact phrase matches and all-token matches are not represented as clear ranking tiers.

**Desired behavior:** Active-locale Common Name exact phrase matches rank first for multi-word queries, followed by active-locale Common Names containing all query tokens, followed by broader partial matches. This should generalize across locales through the same normalization/search-name data used by the whole-token slice.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog query normalization
- Generated Species Catalog search-name index or equivalent indexed data
- Relevance ordering used by the Species Catalog Workbench

**Out of scope:** Do not alter short-query policy, fallback-language tiering, or explicit non-name sort behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A search for French `lin commun` ranks the exact active-locale Common Name phrase first.
- [ ] Active-locale Common Names containing all query tokens rank ahead of broader prefix-only or single-token matches.
- [ ] Multi-word queries continue to support pagination and structured filters.
- [ ] Normalization behavior, including diacritics when already supported by Species Catalog search, is covered by focused tests.
- [ ] Explicit non-name sorts are unaffected.
close_reason: Implemented multi-word active-locale Common Name relevance tiers: exact phrase before all-token matches before broader BM25/taxonomy matches, with diacritic-normalized query tokens, focused fixture and planner tests, docs update, v9 latency harness run, cargo fmt check, cargo check, clippy, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Decide Species Catalog Workbench sort presentation for best match
description:
## Slice Summary
Choose how the Species Catalog Workbench should present effective best-match ranking while the user enters text: keep implicit relevance under Name, show Best match, remove explicit Relevance, or another product choice.

## User Value
The visible sort control accurately communicates why text search results are ordered by relevance.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the UI change in this decision bead. Do not change backend ranking.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Decide how the Species Catalog Workbench sort UI should present best-match relevance during text search.

**Current behavior:** The Workbench can effectively rank by relevance during text search while visible sort controls may suggest a different or overly specific sort, such as name order. That can make the result ordering feel inconsistent.

**Desired behavior:** A human decision records the user-facing sort semantics for empty text, active text search, explicit non-name sorts, and whether a visible Best Match/Relevance option should exist. Downstream implementation should know exactly what the control displays and how it maps to backend sort requests.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench SearchBar and SortSelect behavior
- Species Catalog search sort request
- UI copy and localization keys

**Out of scope:** Do not implement UI or backend changes in this decision bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The decision records the visible sort label and default behavior when no search text is active.
- [ ] The decision records the visible sort label and default behavior when text search is active.
- [ ] The decision records how explicit non-name sorts interact with text search relevance.
- [ ] The decision records whether a visible Best Match/Relevance option exists and what copy it uses.
- [ ] The decision gives enough detail for an agent to update UI behavior and localization without asking a follow-up product question.
notes: Decision: The Species Catalog Workbench should present text-search ranking as Best match, not Relevance. Empty text remains browse mode: the visible default sort is Name and the backend request uses Sort::Name unless the user has selected another explicit sort. When active text has at least two normalized characters and the user has not explicitly chosen a sort for that text search, the visible sort is Best match and the backend request uses Sort::Relevance. Best match should be a visible option using the copy key currently represented by plantDb.sortRelevance, but the English label should be Best match and all locales should be updated with equivalent user-facing copy rather than the technical word Relevance. Explicit sorts remain available during text search. If the user selects Family, Height, Hardiness, Growth rate, or Name while text is active, that selected sort overrides Best match and the backend request sends the selected Sort value. Clearing text returns the automatic default to Name; implementation may keep a separate auto/default state so a text-triggered Best match default does not permanently overwrite the user's browse sort. One-character text follows the broad-query policy and should not show Best match results because no backend text search is issued.
close_reason: Recorded sort-presentation decision: empty browse defaults to Name, active text defaults to visible Best match backed by Sort::Relevance, explicit user-selected sorts override best-match ranking, and localization should use user-facing Best match copy. Decision-only bead; tests not run.

title: Decide short-query and count behavior for broad Species Catalog searches
description:
## Slice Summary
Choose the product and performance policy for one-character and very broad Species Catalog text searches, including whether exact total counts must be returned immediately.

## User Value
The team can make search feel snappy without surprising designers or hiding too much feedback for broad searches.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the chosen policy in this decision bead. Do not change ranking tiers here.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Decide product policy for very short and broad Species Catalog searches before implementing snappy broad-query behavior.

**Current behavior:** One-character and broad queries can generate many Species Catalog candidates. The expected behavior for minimum query length, exact count timing, stale result retention, and user-visible loading/copy is not written down as a durable product decision.

**Desired behavior:** A human decision records how very short and broad searches should behave in the Species Catalog Workbench, including whether counts are exact/immediate, delayed, capped, or omitted. Downstream implementation should not need to reopen the product question.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench search input and count display
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Pagination and loading-state behavior

**Out of scope:** Do not implement backend or UI changes in this decision bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The decision records the intended behavior for one-character queries.
- [ ] The decision records whether first-page result counts must be exact and immediate for broad queries.
- [ ] The decision records any user-visible loading, stale-result, or count-copy behavior needed in the Species Catalog Workbench.
- [ ] The decision states enough constraints for an agent to implement broad-query behavior without asking a follow-up product question.
notes:
Decision: Species Catalog Workbench text search should treat normalized one-character queries as too broad and should not issue a backend text-search request for them. The Workbench should clear the current text-search results for that one-character intent, hide the count, and use the existing no-results state rather than adding new instructional copy. Empty text remains normal browse mode and should still load the first page with an exact count.

For active text searches with at least two normalized characters, first-page responsiveness is more important than an immediate exact total. The first-page request should be allowed to omit exact total counting for text searches; when total count is omitted or unknown, SearchBar should hide the count chip. Pagination remains driven by next_cursor, not total_estimate. Structured-filter-only browsing and empty-text browsing may continue to request exact counts immediately.

While a debounced or in-flight text search is pending, previously committed results should remain visible until the new request commits or resolves to the one-character empty state. Loading state may still be exposed through existing busy indicators. Downstream implementation should preserve explicit non-name sort behavior and should not change relevance tiers in this policy slice.
close_reason: Recorded broad-query policy: one-character text searches are too broad and should not hit backend search; active text first-page requests may omit exact counts and hide the count chip while preserving cursor pagination and committed-result stability. No code tests required for decision-only bead.

title: Demote fallback-language and taxonomy matches below active-locale names
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Species Catalog relevance explicitly prefer active-locale Common Names, then fallback-language Common Names, then Canonical Name, family, and genus matches.

## User Value
Search results match user expectations in non-English locales without hiding useful Canonical Name or taxonomy matches.

## Out of Scope
Do not remove Canonical Name search. Do not change display-name fallback behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Demote fallback-language and taxonomy matches below active-locale Common Name matches in relevance ordering.

**Current behavior:** Common Names from fallback languages and taxonomy fields can intermix too early with active-locale Common Name matches. That makes localized search feel less intuitive when users search in their current UI language.

**Desired behavior:** Relevance uses explicit tiers: active-locale Common Name matches first, fallback-language Common Name matches next, then Canonical Name/family/genus matches, then broad long-token/prefix matches. Canonical Name search should still work; it should just stop outranking strong active-locale Common Name hits.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search locale and fallback-name data
- Canonical Name, family, and genus text matching
- Relevance ordering used by the Species Catalog Workbench

**Out of scope:** Do not change which names are displayed in results or alter Common Name fallback display rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Active-locale Common Name matches rank ahead of fallback-language Common Name matches for relevance-sorted searches.
- [ ] Fallback-language Common Name matches remain discoverable when the active locale has no useful match.
- [ ] Canonical Name, genus, and family searches still return relevant plants.
- [ ] Tests cover at least one non-English locale where fallback/taxonomy matches previously competed with active-locale names.
- [ ] Result display fallback behavior is unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented explicit relevance tiers for active-locale Common Name matches, fallback English Common Name matches, then BM25/taxonomy matches. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::query_builder; cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::plant_db::search; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Add Species Catalog search relevance fixtures and latency harness
description:
## Slice Summary
Create durable Species Catalog search relevance fixtures and a lightweight latency harness that exercises representative active-locale Common Name searches and broad short queries.

## User Value
Maintainers can verify that relevance work actually improves cases such as French `lin`, `lin commun`, and `lind` while keeping first-page search responsive.

## Out of Scope
Do not change ranking behavior in this slice. Do not redesign the Species Catalog Workbench UI.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** task
**Summary:** Add representative Species Catalog search relevance fixtures and a repeatable latency harness before changing ranking.

**Current behavior:** Species Catalog search has limited durable coverage for relevance cases such as French `lin`, multi-token Common Names, and broad short queries. Relevance and latency can regress without a focused signal.

**Desired behavior:** Maintainers have fixtures and a harness that exercise active-locale Common Names, multi-token Common Names, fallback/taxonomy behavior, and broad queries. The harness should separate first-page list latency from total-count latency so future ranking changes can be judged without guesswork.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Generated Species Catalog database and search index
- Species Catalog Workbench search behavior

**Out of scope:** Do not change ranking behavior, search UI behavior, or generated database schema in this slice unless needed only to make the harness runnable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Fixtures cover French `lin`, `lin commun`, `lind`/`lindleya`, and at least one English Common Name case.
- [ ] The harness reports first-page list latency and total-count latency separately for representative queries.
- [ ] The harness runs safely when the bundled Species Catalog database exists and fails or skips with a clear message when it is unavailable.
- [ ] Existing search behavior is unchanged except for added tests or measurement utilities.
close_reason: Added Species Catalog search relevance fixtures for French lin/lin commun/lind and English apple, added an ignored manual latency harness reporting list and count timings separately, documented the harness command, and verified with focused search tests, manual harness run, cargo fmt check, cargo check, clippy, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Align Species Catalog Workbench sort UI with effective ranking

## User Value

## Out of Scope

## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature

**Desired behavior:** The Workbench sort UI implements the approved presentation policy. The visible control state, localized copy, and backend sort request should agree for empty search, active text search, and explicit non-name sorts.

- Species Catalog search sort request
- UI localization files

**Out of scope:** Do not change backend relevance ranking in this UI alignment slice.

- [ ] Visible sort state matches the effective backend sort request for active text search.

Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- src/__tests__/plant-search-session.test.ts src/__tests__/sort-select.test.tsx; cd desktop/web && npm test -- src/__tests__/plant-db-controller.test.ts src/__tests__/results-list.test.tsx; cd desktop/web && npm test -- src/__tests__/plant-search-session.test.ts src/__tests__/sort-select.test.tsx src/__tests__/frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Deepen Species Catalog search planner seams
description:
## Slice Summary
Simplify the Species Catalog backend search planner by separating text normalization, relevance tiers, structured filters, projection, and pagination into clearer seams.

## User Value
Future search changes become reviewable and less likely to degrade relevance, filtering, or pagination performance.

## Out of Scope
Do not introduce new ranking behavior except preserving the behavior already shipped by prerequisite slices. Do not change the shared SpeciesFilter request shape.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** task
**Summary:** Refactor Species Catalog search planner seams so relevance rules can evolve without fragile SQL assembly.

**Current behavior:** Search planning responsibilities are tightly coupled: query normalization, relevance ranking, filters, projection, pagination, and count/list predicate sharing are difficult to reason about independently.

**Desired behavior:** The search planner has clear internal seams for text normalization, relevance tiers, structured filters, projection, pagination, and count/list predicate reuse. The refactor should preserve behavior from the completed ranking slices while making future relevance changes safer and easier to test.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Species Catalog Filter predicate construction
- Relevance ordering and pagination
- Count/list query consistency

**Out of scope:** Do not introduce new user-visible ranking behavior in this refactor.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Search planner code separates normalization, relevance-tier construction, structured filters, projection, pagination, and count/list predicate reuse into clear units.
- [ ] Existing relevance and filter tests continue to pass without weakening assertions.
- [ ] Count and list queries continue to share equivalent filter semantics.
- [ ] The refactor does not introduce new user-visible ranking behavior.
- [ ] Agent docs are updated if database/search planner ownership or commands change.
close_reason: Deepened Species Catalog search planner seams without changing ranking behavior: text normalization, relevance tiers/token joins, shared predicates, pagination, and projection now live in separate query_builder modules while SpeciesSearchPlan remains the public interface. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::query_builder; cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::plant_db::search; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; git diff --check.

title: Deepen Species Catalog Filter and Design Session modules
description:
## Slice Summary
Track the two approved architecture improvements from the architecture review: deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior and deepen Design Session lifecycle behavior.

## User Value
The codebase gains locality and leverage around two high-friction seams without broad rewrites or contract churn.

## Out of Scope
Do not pursue Target planning projection, canvas runtime surface changes, Site Adaptation refactors, broad folder renames, shared SpeciesFilter contract replacement, or autosave rewrites unless a child bead proves they block the tracer bullets.
design:
## Design Brief
Track the approved architecture improvement as a narrow, resumable epic.

The epic contains two tracer bullets:
- Deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior with `life_cycle`.
- Deepen Design Session lifecycle behavior by unifying attached and detached transition selection.

Architectural decisions already made:
- Keep `SpeciesFilter` plus `extra` stable as the external shared request contract for now.
- Normalize Species Catalog Filter behavior internally before considering a shared contract replacement.
- Keep generated filter metadata declarative; adapters own executable behavior.
- Use `life_cycle` as the first Species Catalog Filter tracer because it proves bespoke predicate behavior.
- Use **Design Session** as the domain term for the active Design runtime context.
- Let Design Session choose attached versus detached canvas behavior internally.
- Leave autosave out of the first Design Session slice except to preserve current behavior.

Discarded candidates from the review:
- Target planning projection.
- Canvas runtime surface changes.
- Site Adaptation rules.

Do not revive discarded candidates unless a child bead proves they are blocking a tracer bullet.
acceptance_criteria:
- Both child tracer beads are created and linked under this epic.
- Each child bead records scope, design decisions, acceptance criteria, and out-of-scope boundaries.
- `CONTEXT.md` contains durable vocabulary for Species Catalog Filter and Design Session.
- After each child implementation lands, update this epic or create follow-up beads with what the code proved.
close_reason: Completed both approved architecture tracer slices: Species Catalog Filter behavior with life_cycle landed via PR #12, and Design Session transition lifecycle landed via PR #13. Verified local focused/full frontend gates and CI for both branches.

title: Unify Design Session transition attached and detached paths
description:
## Slice Summary
Use Design transition selection as a tracer bullet for a deeper Design Session lifecycle module: callers request a transition and the module decides whether a canvas runtime is attached.

## User Value
Open, new, template, queued, and mount-existing Design flows become harder to call out of order because dirty guard, normalization, dirty baselines, workflow install, canvas chrome, and zoom sequence live behind one Design Session seam.

## Out of Scope
Do not rename document-session folders, rewrite autosave, change .canopi contracts, move all save composition, or touch Target planning, MapLibre, or canvas runtime surfaces unless a test proves they block this transition seam.
design:
## Design Brief
Deepen Design Session lifecycle behavior using attached/detached transition selection as the tracer bullet.

Callers should request a Design transition. The Design Session implementation should decide whether a canvas runtime is attached and apply the correct attached or detached path internally. Dirty guard, save-before-replace, normalization, state replacement, dirty baseline reset, workflow install, canvas chrome, and zoom ordering should become harder to call out of order.

Use this slice to reduce the current caller-visible split between `transitionDocument()` and `transitionDetachedDocument()`. Keep autosave out of the first slice except where existing call sites must keep working.

## Relevant Files
- `CONTEXT.md`
- `docs/agent/document-lifecycle.md`
- `docs/agent/canvas-runtime.md`
- `desktop/web/src/app/document-session/actions.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/app/document-session/transition.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/app/document-session/runtime.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/app/document-session/use-canvas-document-session.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/app/document-session/workflows.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/state/design.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/canvas/session.ts`
- focused document-session tests

## Notes
- Use the domain term Design Session in docs and handoff. Existing implementation paths may keep `document-session` naming during the tracer bullet to avoid noisy renames.
- Do not change Design authority: canvas scene state remains in `SceneStore`; non-canvas Design state remains in the document layer.
- Talk to canvas through `CanvasDocumentSurface`, not `SceneCanvasRuntime`.
acceptance_criteria:
- Open/new/template/queued/mount-existing flows request transitions through one Design Session transition interface.
- The Design Session implementation decides attached versus detached behavior internally.
- Dirty guard and save-before-replace behavior are preserved.
- Attached transitions still run canvas-specific post-load behavior such as canvas chrome and `zoomToFit()`.
- Detached transitions avoid canvas-only calls while preserving normalization, current Design state replacement, dirty baseline reset, and workflow installation.
- Autosave behavior is preserved and not rewritten in this slice.
- Focused tests cover attached and detached transition behavior through the new seam.
- Required quality gates are run and documented.
close_reason: Unified Design Session transition selection: callers now use transitionDocument while the transition module resolves the attached CanvasDocumentSurface or applies a detached document-state transition, preserving dirty guard/save-before-replace behavior, queued loads, attached canvas chrome/zoom/history behavior, and detached no-canvas behavior. Verification: npm test -- document-session-transition document-actions use-canvas-document-session; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior with life cycle tracer
description:
## Slice Summary
Use the life_cycle Species Catalog Filter as a tracer bullet for a deeper filter behavior module while keeping the external SpeciesFilter plus extra request contract stable.

## User Value
Adding or changing Species Catalog Filters becomes safer because UI behavior, active filter formatting, request normalization, option loading, and Rust predicate building stop leaking fixed-filter rules across callers.

## Out of Scope
Do not replace the shared SpeciesFilter contract, migrate every filter in one pass, change user-visible filter behavior, add dependencies, or rework Species Catalog search pagination.
design:
## Design Brief
Deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior using `life_cycle` as the tracer bullet.

The external shared request contract remains `SpeciesFilter` plus `extra`; this bead normalizes fixed and dynamic filter behavior behind internal frontend and Rust seams. Generated filter metadata should stay declarative. Executable behavior belongs in adapters that interpret metadata.

Use `life_cycle` because it is user-facing filter behavior backed by bespoke Rust predicates over `is_annual`, `is_biennial`, and `is_perennial`, not a single SQL column. The slice should prove that UI placement is separate from predicate behavior and that a Species Catalog Filter domain key is separate from any SQL column implementation.

Preserve current user-visible behavior. Keep the change narrow enough that follow-up beads can migrate `sun_tolerances`, `soil_tolerances`, `climate_zones`, `edibility_min`, and `nitrogen_fixer` after the seam proves itself.

## Relevant Files
- `CONTEXT.md`
- `docs/agent/database.md`
- `docs/agent/frontend-patterns.md`
- `common-types/plant-filter-fields.json`
- `bindings-gen/src/main.rs`
- `desktop/web/src/generated/plant-filter-fields.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/app/plant-browser/plant-filter-model.ts`
- `desktop/web/src/components/plant-db/FilterStrip.tsx`
- `desktop/web/src/components/plant-db/ActiveChips.tsx`
- `desktop/src/db/query_builder/filters.rs`
- `desktop/src/db/plant_db/filters.rs`
- `desktop/src/db/plant_filter_fields.rs`
- focused frontend and backend tests

## Notes
- Do not put executable business logic directly in generated files.
- Keep generated metadata safe and declarative.
- Prefer a vertical slice over migrating all Species Catalog Filters.
acceptance_criteria:
- `life_cycle` remains accepted through the existing `SpeciesFilter` request shape.
- `life_cycle` is represented as a first-class Species Catalog Filter behavior in generated/declarative metadata or an adapter-owned registry.
- Frontend filter strip and active chips no longer hardcode `life_cycle` as a one-off special row/chip outside the Species Catalog Filter module.
- Rust query predicate construction routes `life_cycle` through the Species Catalog Filter behavior adapter while preserving the `Annual`/`Biennial`/`Perennial` boolean-column semantics.
- Existing `life_cycle` search behavior is preserved, including no-op behavior for unknown values.
- Focused frontend tests cover rendering/active-chip behavior through the new seam.
- Focused Rust tests cover generated/adapted `life_cycle` predicate behavior.
- Required quality gates are run and documented.
close_reason: Deepened Species Catalog Filter behavior with a life_cycle tracer: frontend strip and active-chip behavior now flows through plantFilterCatalog/species-catalog-filters, and Rust life_cycle predicates route through query_builder/species_catalog_filters while preserving boolean-column and unknown-value no-op semantics. Verification: npm test -- plant-filter-model active-chips filter-strip; cargo test -p canopi-desktop query_builder; npx tsc --noEmit; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace.
